mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% represents an extremely accommodative monetary stance. This signals persistent low discount rates, which mechanically increase the present value of future equity cash flows and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 delivers negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 6.2% exceeds the typical natural rate, signaling labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending. This adverse labor condition forecasts negative corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5%, hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts (1580K) and retail sales ($568.2B) indicates robust consumer and residential investment demand. This broad-based demand momentum supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is negative in more than half of days when both HOUST>1500 and RSAFS>550000, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding (year-over-year growth > 0%), it signals increasing supply capacity and economic output. This expansion regime supports corporate earnings and is positive for S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. In contraction regimes, the signal would reverse sign.
failure_condition: If during periods of INDPRO yoy > 0%, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative on average (mean < 0), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal that both supply and labor demand are strengthening. This joint expansion supports durable economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month increase and PAYEMS month-over-month increase
failure_condition: If the mean forward 20-day return when both series rise month-over-month is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at $18.78 trillion (chained 2017 dollars) reflects a large and mature economy. The absolute level of GDP, when adjusted for trend, provides a baseline valuation anchor; higher GDP levels imply higher potential earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If over a 3-year rolling window, the rank correlation between lagged GDP level and 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (Rank IC < 0.02), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate combined with rising payroll employment signals improving labor market conditions and increasing household income. This combination is a strong leading indicator for consumer spending and positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines month-over-month and PAYEMS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return is negative in > 55% of events when both triggers are met, the interaction hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at $568.2 billion exceed the $560 billion threshold, indicating robust consumer spending. Strong retail sales often lead to positive earnings surprises and near-term stock gains over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the mean 5-day S&P 500 return after RSAFS > 560000 is not greater than the unconditional mean return (one-sided test p < 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.
